item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Slide down the railing! Slide down the railing! (This is why I am not part of the research staff)

Anyway, much more creative than I was expecting. +1

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license as it comes from Parks Canada which prohibits its images to be used "for the purposes of commercial redistribution without prior written permission from the Government of Canada's copyright administrator". With lack of permission displayed, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love the article and the concept. Easy +1 from me.

However, the cause of death for D-1145 should be amended. Being caught on Mars without a spacesuit would not cause their organs to rupture due to depressurization because the skin is sufficient enough of a protective layer to prevent this from happening. D-1145 would definitely have suffocated, frozen, or roasted to death long before the sudden lack of pressure killed him. The whole "sucked out of an airlock" thing is pretty much pure science-fiction to make space movies scarier. While it would be a traumatic experience for sure, comic book-esque depressurization simply wouldn't happen. The air would be sucked out of their lungs, or it would have exploded outwards, causing them to collapse (or potentially rupture). That's about it though. Collapsed or ruptured lungs end up suffocating you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Almost reminds me of the paining that makes all step-downs a cliff jump. I can't remember the number….

item.title: JUNIOR STAFF POST OPEN - Crosslink Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer expired, edits implemented.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Proposed Edits
- Proposed Edits

A crosslink is being proposed to Research Site-45 as it is referenced in SCP-2403's containment procedures, and the dossier page for Research Site-45 links back to SCP-2403. In addition, the author of SCP-2403 is also the author of the dossier page for Research Site-45.

Editing this:

The east stairwell of Research Site-45, between the 1st and 3rd floors, is to be closed to personnel indefinitely.

To this:

The east stairwell of Research Site-45, between the 1st and 3rd floors, is to be closed to personnel indefinitely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The test logs are excellent and it felt nicely old-school in the best possible way. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this as an anomalous incarnation of the mass human experience of getting a stomach-drop from misjudging the last step. Seconding that the logs are really well-written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1, and I really like the experiments

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Been meaning to get to this one ever since I saw "The Air Stair" on the lc list in the IRC. Let me start off by saying that this has a great title.

While this is a rather simple anomaly, it has an old school vibe that a lot of others have already commented on. Additionally, how the anomaly ramps up as more and more people jump from further and further distances is highly entertaining.

+1. Just because a concept is simple, doesn't mean it can't be both fun and on certain level terrifying. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's my theory as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Watch that last step. It's a doozy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought he fell into a black hole

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Done really well and very interesting, but the principle feels just a little too similar to 1753, so I'm hesitant to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the simplicity of this.

+1 for a reverse stairway to heaven

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I also have this headcanon. Also everyone's super chill about it (except D-class, obvs). "Ugh, sometimes this stairwell drops you on Mars so I have to avoid it and I guess my walk to the lab takes five extra minutes now, sigh."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A very simple anomaly in a thoroughly mundane, Foundation-local place, with some fun ramifications. I do kind of think that the last three tests are the only ones that need to be security-locked, but that's not enough to keep me from upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Naw. So far as I can tell, the higher you jump from, the further the drop. So you start with a step, a cliff. 3 steps, a really big cliff. A lot of steps, a really drop. The last log is when they tell him to jump from the very top of the stairs, so it should logically be the very longest drop.

My guess is that they dropped him in a star or something and that he was instantaneously vaporized/ blown away so that when he returned, all that was left was the radiation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just to be clear, the staircase got fed up with their cheating, so he turned the D-Class into radiation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What a perfect way to cap off the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You bring up a good point. I'll retool the last logs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the old-school vibe here, and it meshes well with a personal headcanon that this sort of minor anomaly pops up at Foundation sites now and then as a side effect of containing so many anomalous things in one place. This one just happens to be terrifying and potentially hazardous rather than being the bathroom they keep locked and no one talks about.

+1, but one nitpick: D-Class are expendable, but not disposable- even on the coldest level, if they live you can use them for another test. On that logic, the d-class after test 4441-1-7 should totally have had a spacesuit, or at least an oxygen tank and a survival suit.

Also, what if parachutes?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, I thought I'd seen this on the site, I guess I actually saw it in a sandbox.

Anyway +1 for a simple but compelling idea, well-executed
